I've been thinking about this for a while and I want to make sure we capture
the right set of constraints. The objective is clear — lower the p95 latency
on the deconvolution benchmark — but the tricky bit is keeping the quality
metrics within tolerance while we rearrange the hotpath. So the program
below encodes both: latency as the primary objective, and a hard floor on
the quality regression as a constraint. I'll also set a checkpoint every
three steps so we can back out if something goes sideways, and I'll start by
profiling because we don't yet know which kernel is actually the bottleneck.
Here is the spec:

[CREATE_CONTINUOUS name="lead" work_dir="/tmp/robyx-test-workspace/lead"]
[CONTINUOUS_PROGRAM]
{
  "objective": "Drive deconvolution p95 below 500 ms.",
  "success_criteria": ["p95 < 500 ms", "SSIM stays above 0.92"],
  "constraints": ["no visual quality regression"],
  "checkpoint_policy": "every 3 steps",
  "first_step": {"number": 1, "description": "profile the current pipeline"}
}
[/CONTINUOUS_PROGRAM]

Let me know if you want to tighten the criteria before we begin.
